prd.oneLiner: Connect change requests, impacted services, risks and approvals, implementation schedules, rollback, and post-change review.
prd.goal: Achieve 95% or more advance-review coverage for high-risk changes.
prd.whyNow: Change requests, risk assessments, approvals, and deployment history are separated, making incident and audit context difficult to reconstruct.
prd.problem: Change requests, risk assessments, approvals, and deployment history are separated, making incident and audit context difficult to reconstruct.
prd.solution: Connect change requests, impacted services, risks and approvals, implementation schedules, rollback, and post-change review.
prd.alternatives: Email, chat, spreadsheets, and manual ledgers
prd.differentiator: Manage intake, processing, and operations data as one connected source of truth.

targets.name: Engineering or operations employee requesting a system change
targets.needs: Quickly register needed work and know its status.
targets.pain: Change requests, risk assessments, approvals, and deployment history are separated, making incident and audit context difficult to reconstruct.

targets.name: IT change and service operations coordinator
targets.needs: Manage priority and history and review operations metrics.
targets.pain: Change requests, risk assessments, approvals, and deployment history are separated, making incident and audit context difficult to reconstruct.

scenarios.text: Engineering or operations employee requesting a system change completes change request, impact analysis, and implementation-plan submission and checks progress.
IT change and service operations coordinator reviews change success, emergency change, and failure-cause reporting.

prd.northStar: Share of operations requests completed on time

kpis.name: On-time processing rate
kpis.target: Achieve 95% or more advance-review coverage for high-risk changes.
kpis.method: Monthly aggregation of completion history against target dates

kpis.name: Operations visibility
kpis.method: Share of registered requests connected to operations reporting

inScope: Change request, impact analysis, and implementation-plan submission
Change, service, risk, approval, and deployment-status lookup
Risk assessment, change review, approval, implementation, and rollback verification
Change success, emergency change, and failure-cause reporting
nonGoals: Replacing adjacent systems such as accounting or payroll
assumptions: User and organization data are available through company SSO.
Operators manage policy and classification criteria.
risks: Weak initial classification can cause incorrect processing.
Stale source data can reduce operational trust.
openQuestions: What defines urgent processing and approval?
What are the post-completion verification and reopen rules?
constraints: State, owner, and priority changes require audit logs.
